context.description: A nonfiction chapter section explains the hidden emotional system as an ancient, largely unconscious mechanism that drives avoidance of pain and pursuit of pleasure. It uses examples such as spiders, puppies, and an armed robbery to illustrate how emotional cascades are triggered beyond conscious control.
normalization_notes: Detected as a body-content chapter section from continuous prose and conceptual framing.
Source language is German; normalized surface values are in English.

fields.section_heading: The Wonderful World of the Hidden Supercomputer – Emotional Cascades

rows.paragraph_text: The text argues that emotions are driven by an ancient internal system that seeks pleasure and avoids pain, often outside conscious control.
It uses examples like a spider, a puppy, and an armed robbery to show how emotional cascades can begin before a person understands what is happening.
